Core hospitality tools

Start with the part of service that needs to work better now.

Available now

QR menu to WhatsApp order—without the confusing bits.

Give customers a clear mobile menu, collect table or delivery details, and send a structured order directly to your WhatsApp for confirmation.

Mini POS

Order punching, simple tables, KOT-friendly tickets and an uncomplicated cashier view for smaller restaurant operations.

Coming soon

POS + QR menu, linked

Bring dine-in QR orders and counter orders into one view, so the kitchen and billing desk stay aligned.

Coming soon

Food cost & pricing calculator

Calculate recipe cost, packaging, platform fees, discounts, taxes and your target margin before setting the menu price.

Coming soon

Direct ordering & local delivery

A branded ordering web app for direct customers, helping you take repeat orders without relying only on marketplace commissions.

Hospitality roadmap

Start with QR ordering. Add connected tools when they are ready.

ForgeArc begins with a clear QR-to-WhatsApp ordering flow. POS, pricing and direct-order tools are planned as the next parts of the hospitality stack.

QR ordering is available for deployment today.
Future products will remain mobile-first and hospitality-specific.
Every next tool will be shaped around real counter, floor and kitchen flow.

How a ForgeArc build moves

Clear scope.
Useful launch.

We start with the operational bottleneck, not a giant feature list. The aim is a useful first system your team can run confidently.

01

Map the current flow

We identify where orders, calculations, bookings, follow-ups or staff handoffs are becoming slow, messy or inconsistent.

Discovery
02

Build the useful first version

We prioritise the flow your staff and customers will actually use, with a mobile-first interface and straightforward operating logic.

Build
03

Refine after real service

After the system is used in a real operation, we add only the next features that prove useful.

Improve
Start with one bottleneck

Let’s make service easier to run.

Send your requirement directly on WhatsApp. Include the service you are considering and a short note about the current problem.
